sections: What Counts as Non-Fragile Packing — and Why It’s the Smart Place to Start

Non-fragile packing covers everything in your home that won’t shatter if a box is set down a little firmly: paperbacks and hardbacks, jumpers and coats, bed linen and towels, children’s toys, shoes, and the contents of the garage and loft. These are the items that make up the bulk of any move by volume, yet they are also the simplest and safest things to pack yourself. By focusing your own time on this lower-risk category, you keep full control of your possessions while still removing the most labour-intensive part of moving day from the equation. Our crews then arrive to a home that is already largely boxed, labelled and ready to load. If you’d rather hand everything over instead, our full packing service takes care of the entire property from start to finish, but for most households a sensible split between DIY non-fragile work and professional fragile packing strikes the best balance of cost and care.
How a Part-Pack Saves You Money on Removal Day

The single biggest variable in any removals quote is time on site, and packing is where the hours quietly add up. When you handle the non-fragile items in the weeks before your move, you shorten the loading window, reduce the number of crew hours billed, and often make a same-day move possible where a fully managed pack might have stretched into two. As a family-run Sussex company we have packed thousands of homes since 2016, and we can tell you exactly which jobs reward the DIY effort and which are better left to us.

  • Books, DVDs and media into small boxes you can fill at your own pace each evening
  • Clothing folded into medium cartons, or hung straight into our wardrobe boxes on the day
  • Bed linen, towels and soft furnishings, which double as natural padding inside larger boxes
  • Toys, games and sports kit sorted, decluttered and sealed well ahead of time
  • Garage, shed and loft contents grouped so nothing is forgotten at the back of a shelf

Tackling these categories yourself can meaningfully lower the total on your quote. Use our free moving quote form to compare a part-pack against a full service, or review our transparent removals pricing to see how the hours translate into cost.
Pairing Your Non-Fragile Packing With Our Fragile Specialists

The reason a part-pack works so well is that it lets each task go to whoever does it best. You know your books and your wardrobe better than anyone, so packing them is straightforward. Glassware, china, mirrors, artwork, electronics and treasured antiques are a different matter entirely, and these are exactly the items where a dropped box turns into an insurance claim. That is why so many of our customers combine their own non-fragile boxing with our fragile packing service, where trained packers wrap delicate pieces in tissue, bubble and purpose-made cartons. As a LAPADA member we handle fine art and antiques to a recognised standard, and every move is fully insured with £10m public liability cover, so the items that matter most travel under professional protection while you keep the simpler work in hand.
The Materials We Recommend for Self-Packing

Good packing starts with good materials, and the most common mistake we see is supermarket boxes that buckle under the weight of a few hardbacks. Proper double-walled removal cartons hold their shape, stack squarely in the van and protect their contents from the load above. We supply everything you need through our packing materials shop so your home pack is consistent and secure rather than a patchwork of odd-sized boxes.

  • Small book boxes that stay light enough to lift safely once full
  • Large boxes for bulky, lightweight items such as bedding, pillows and duvets
  • Wardrobe boxes that let clothes travel hung and crease-free
  • Packing tape, marker pens and box labels for fast, clear identification
  • Plain newsprint and bubble wrap for any borderline items you’d rather cushion

If you under-order, our team can drop additional materials to you across West Sussex, East Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire and Kent, so you’re never caught short the night before the move.
Labelling and Loading: The System That Speeds Up the Day

A well-packed home only pays off if the crew can read it. We ask self-packing customers to label every box on the top and on one side with the destination room and a one-line note of the contents — ‘Kitchen — pans’ rather than simply ‘Kitchen’. This lets our team load the van logically and, more importantly, place each box straight into the right room at the other end, which is where most of the time and frustration is saved. Number your boxes per room and keep a quick tally so nothing is left behind, and stack sealed cartons in a single accessible spot ahead of the move so loading flows without interruption. These small habits, repeated across a full house, can shave an hour or more off the day. Once you’re unpacked at the far end, our full unpacking service can flatten and remove all that cardboard so you’re not left with a hallway of empty boxes.
Know What to Leave to the Professionals

Self-packing is about working with us, not replacing us, and there are some things we always recommend handing over. Heavy, awkward or valuable items — the chest of tools in the garage, the flat-screen television, the framed prints up the stairwell — are best left in the van and in our hands. Our crews carry the right equipment, trolleys and protective coverings, and they are trained in safe lifting so that a back is never put at risk over a box of paving slabs from the shed. For smaller or single-item moves, our man and van service offers the same care on a flexible scale, while a full household relocation is covered end to end by our house removals service. As a Checkatrade-verified, family-run firm recommended by Fine & Country, Justin Lloyd and Mansell McTaggart, our advice is always the same: pack what is safe and simple, and let us carry the rest.
faqs: Which items should I pack myself and which should Wolves Removals pack?

Pack the non-fragile, lower-risk items yourself — books, clothes, linen, towels, toys, shoes and most garage or loft contents. Leave anything fragile, heavy or high-value to our trained crews, including glassware, china, mirrors, artwork, electronics and antiques. Combining your own non-fragile packing with our fragile packing service gives you the best balance of saving money and protecting your most precious possessions.
Will doing my own non-fragile packing actually reduce my removals quote?

Yes. Packing is one of the most time-consuming parts of any move, and most quotes are driven by hours on site. By boxing your own non-fragile items in advance, you shorten loading time and reduce crew hours, which lowers the overall cost. Request a free moving quote or view our pricing to compare a part-pack with our full service.
Can you supply boxes and packing materials if I’m packing myself?

Absolutely. We supply double-walled removal cartons, wardrobe boxes, tape, bubble wrap, newsprint and labels through our packing materials shop, and we can deliver across West Sussex, East Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire and Kent. Proper materials keep your boxes strong, square and easy to stack, which protects your belongings and speeds up the move.
How should I label my boxes to make moving day faster?

Label every box on the top and one side with its destination room and a short note of the contents, for example ‘Bedroom 2 — bedding’. This lets our team load logically and place each box directly into the correct room at your new home, saving real time. Stack your sealed boxes in one accessible spot before we arrive, and our crew handles the rest.
